Title: A flavin-dependent monooxygenase catalyzes the initial step in cyanogenic glycoside synthesis in ferns

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Phlebodium aureum; Pteridium aquilinum; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class B flavin-dependent monooxygenase (FMO); FAD- and NADPH-binding monooxygenase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Initial oxime-forming step of phenylalanine-derived cyanogenic glycoside pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts L-phenylalanine to phenylacetaldoxime via N-hydroxyphenylalanine through sequential N-hydroxylations followed by decarboxylation and dehydration
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate pathway entry intermediate and supplies precursor directly to prunasin/vicianin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control/committed-step enzyme for pathway entry
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Binary chemical defense system releasing toxic hydrogen cyanide upon tissue disruption; also associated generally with reduced nitrogen storage, bud break/flower induction, and reactive oxygen species quenching
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Pteridium aquilinum pinnae/fronds (prunasin); Phlebodium aureum fiddlehead and young pinnae/fronds (vicianin highest in emerging fiddlehead)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables oxime formation from phenylalanine and thereby initiates cyanogenic glycoside biosynthesis in ferns; transient expression produced phenylacetaldoxime in Nicotiana benthamiana and, with downstream almond genes, produced prunasin
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher transcript abundance in high-cyanogenic tissues: 24-fold higher in P. aureum fiddlehead than young pinna; moderately higher in high-prunasin P. aquilinum individual than low-prunasin individual
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected by transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves after agroinfiltration
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-collected high- vs low-prunasin P. aquilinum individuals; tissue type differences in P. aureum; transient agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana; in vitro assays required FAD and NADPH
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative transcriptomics / RNA-seq of de novo assembled transcriptomes from Phlebodium aureum and Pteridium aquilinum; fern whole genome duplication SRA search accession PRJNA422112
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PaqFOS1 and PaFOS1 proteins are ~98% identical despite species divergence ~140 MYA. Recombinant PaqFOS1 was purified as a catalytically active homodimer (~120 kDa native, ~60 kDa monomer on SDS-PAGE). FOS1 showed specificity for phenylalanine and no other oximes/additional products attributable to alternate amino acid substrates were observed. N-hydroxyphenylalanine was detected in vivo in fern tissue and in N. benthamiana expressing FOS1, a metabolite class not previously observed in plants.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering cyanogenic glycoside biosynthesis or oxime production in heterologous systems; soluble FMO alternative to membrane-bound CYP79 may facilitate industrial pathway reconstruction, but deployment requires caution because cyanogenic glycosides release toxic hydrogen cyanide
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FOS1 from two fern species catalyzes the first committed step of cyanogenic glycoside biosynthesis by converting phenylalanine to phenylacetaldoxime. 2. FOS1 acts through an N-hydroxyphenylalanine intermediate, demonstrating a novel plant oxime-forming mechanism independent of CYP79. 3. Expression of FOS1 in Nicotiana benthamiana produced phenylacetaldoxime, and co-expression with almond CYP71AN24 and UGT85A19 reconstituted prunasin production. 4. Recombinant FOS1 is an active homodimeric class B FMO.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Differential transcriptomics; Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS metabolite profiling; co-expression pathway reconstruction with CYP71AN24 and UGT85A19; recombinant protein purification from E. coli; in vitro enzyme assays; detection of in vivo intermediate N-hydroxyphenylalanine
